description_long: This is a round, wide-mouthed ceramic vase with a pronounced, outward-flaring rim and a bulbous body. The surface displays a patchwork of gray, off-white, and brownish tones, with visible restoration lines and areas where the original material has been repaired or reconstructed. Subtle horizontal banding and faint, hand-applied linear patterns are present, especially near the upper portion of the body. The finish is matte and slightly rough, with clear evidence of age and wear, including chips and surface irregularities. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, typical of utilitarian or decorative pottery. This vase would be well-suited for display in a public urban space, such as a park or plaza, where its historical and artisanal character can be appreciated.
description: A restored, wide-mouthed ceramic vase with a bulbous body, matte finish, and visible patchwork of gray, off-white, and brown tones. Subtle banding and hand-applied patterns accentuate its aged, artisanal appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flared rim, patchwork restoration, and faint horizontal banding with hand-applied lines.
description_view_1: This side highlights a large, lighter restoration area and a smoother, less patterned surface.
description_view_2: Back view reveals the most extensive patchwork repairs and a darker, more uniform lower section.
description_view_3: This angle displays a prominent circular patch and more visible surface wear, with subtle banding.
attribute_pattern: subtle horizontal banding and hand-applied lines
